info.name: Rukuni API
info.description: Complete Postman collection for the Rukuni backend API. Set `base_url` (e.g. `http://localhost:8000`) and `auth_token` (JWT) as collection variables before making authenticated requests.

request.description: Simple liveness check. Returns `{ "message": "pong" }`.

Register a new user account. `referral_code` is optional. A verification email is sent after registration. Password must meet Laravel's default strength rules (min 8 chars, mixed case + numbers recommended).

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description: Resend the email verification link. Rate-limited to once every 60 seconds per user.

response.name: 429 Rate Limited

query.description: User ID from the signed verification URL

request.description: Verifies a user's email address using the signed URL sent via email. In practice the full URL is generated by the server and emailed to the user.
Authenticate and receive a JWT token. The test script auto-saves the token to the `auth_token` collection variable.
Request a 6-digit OTP reset code to the user's email. The code expires in 15 minutes (900 seconds).

response.name: 200 Code Sent

request.description: Reset the user's password using the 6-digit OTP received via email. The code must be exactly 6 characters.

response.name: 200 Password Reset

request.description: Invalidate the current JWT token. Requires `auth:api` middleware.
Exchange an existing (or recently expired) JWT for a new one. Does NOT require a valid non-expired token — expired tokens can reach this endpoint.

response.name: 401 Token Expired / Invalid

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's dashboard data: profile, groups (owned + member), suggested groups, pending invitations, and statistics.
Update the authenticated user's profile information (name, mobile, etc.).
Change the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password to be valid.
Add / upgrade the authenticated user's plan.
Get the authenticated user's point balance, transaction history, and streak data.
Redeem the authenticated user's earned points.
Public endpoint. Returns all active plans ordered by price.

response.name: 200 Plans List
response.body:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Starter",
      "slug": "starter",
      "tagline": "Perfect for getting started",
      "price": 0,
      "currency": "NGN",
      "billing": "free_forever",
      "features": ["Up to 5 group members", "1 active group"],
      "max_groups": 1,
      "max_members_per_group": 5,
      "is_active": true
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's currently active plan.
User joins a plan by plan ID. Only `free_forever` plans are self-serve; paid plans must be admin-assigned. Replaces any existing active plan.

response.name: 403 Paid Plan Restriction

request.description: Returns all groups the authenticated user owns or is a member of.
Get a specific group by ID.

Create a new contribution group. Requires the `create_group` plan permission middleware.

**contribution_frequency options:** `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`

- `payment_out_day` (1–31): required when frequency is `monthly`
- `payment_out_weekday` (0–6, Mon=0): required when frequency is `weekly`

The creator is auto-added as admin. All provided `members_emails` are invited (accounts are created for non-existing emails).

response.name: 403 Member Limit Exceeded
422 Validation Error

request.description: Accept a pending group invitation. Requires `requires.plan` middleware. Awards points to the group owner.
Send a join request to a group's admin. Requires `requires.plan` middleware. The group admin receives a notification.
Approve a pending join request for a group. Only the group admin can approve. Requires `requires.plan` middleware.
Reject a pending join request for a group. Only the group admin can reject. Requires `requires.plan` middleware.
Validate a referral code before user signs up. Returns the referrer's name so the UI can display it.
Returns the authenticated user's referral code, stats (active, pending, total_points), earnings overview, milestone progress, and referral history.
Returns the list of users referred by the authenticated user.
Generates a fresh unique referral code for the authenticated user, replacing the old one.

query.description: Filter by notification type (partial match e.g. `group`, `referral`, `login`)

request.description: Get paginated notifications for the authenticated user. Both query params are optional.
Get a single notification by UUID. Automatically marks the notification as read.
Mark a specific notification as read by UUID.
Mark all unread notifications for the authenticated user as read.
Permanently delete a notification by UUID.
Returns all active FAQ categories with their articles. No authentication required.

response.name: 200 FAQ Categories

request.description: Full-text search across all active FAQ articles. Returns up to 20 results. Requires at least 2 characters.

response.name: 200 Search Results

request.description: Get a specific FAQ category and all its active articles by slug. Common slugs: `account`, `groups`, `payments`, `payouts`, `billing`, `technical`.
Submit helpful / not-helpful feedback on a FAQ article. `articleId` is the numeric article ID.
Returns support contact information and SLA details.
Returns all support tickets for the authenticated user with optional filters. Also returns aggregate stats.

Submit a new support ticket.

**category options:** `account`, `groups`, `payments`, `payouts`, `notifications`, `billing`, `technical`, `fraud`

**priority options:** `low`, `medium` (default), `high`, `critical`

`message` must be between 20 and 5000 characters.

response.name: 201 Ticket Created
response.body:
{
  "status": "success",
  "message": "Ticket submitted successfully",
  "ticket": {
    "id": 1,
    "ticket_id": "TK-001",
    "subject": "Payment not reflecting in dashboard",
    "category": "payments",
    "priority": "medium",
    "status": "open",
    "sla": "24h",
    "created_at": "2026-03-28T10:00:00.000000Z"
  }
}

request.description: Get detailed information about a specific ticket including all replies and a timeline. Use the human-readable ticket ID (e.g., `TK-001`), not the numeric database ID.

response.name: 200 Ticket Details
response.body:
{
  "status": "success",
  "ticket": {
    "id": 1,
    "ticket_id": "TK-001",
    "subject": "Payment not reflecting",
    "category": "payments",
    "priority": "medium",
    "status": "open",
    "message": "My payment is not showing...",
    "sla_deadline": "2026-03-29T10:00:00.000000Z",
    "sla_breached": false
  },
  "replies": [],
  "timeline": []
}

request.description: Add a reply to an open ticket. Cannot reply to closed tickets. `message` must be 5–5000 characters.

response.name: 201 Reply Added

request.description: Escalate a ticket to senior support. Auto-upgrades the priority to `high`. Cannot escalate already-escalated or resolved/closed tickets.
Submit feedback on a resolved ticket. Indicate whether the resolution was helpful.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Pro",
  "slug": "pro",
  "tagline": "For serious contributors",
  "price": 2000,
  "currency": "NGN",
  "billing": "monthly",
  "features": [
    "Up to 50 group members",
    "5 active groups",
    "Priority support"
  ],
  "built_for": ["Businesses", "Power users"],
  "max_groups": 5,
  "max_members_per_group": 50,
  "is_active": true
}

request.description:
Create a new subscription plan. Requires `admin` role.

**billing options:** `free_forever`, `monthly`, `yearly`
Update an existing plan by numeric plan ID. All fields are optional (partial update).
Soft-delete (or hard-delete depending on model config) a plan by numeric ID. Requires `admin` role.
